name: Molybdenum Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Molybdenum. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Molybdenum oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength providing good absorption for Molybdenum surface contaminants

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning resolution and energy density

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal from Molybdenum surface

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient contaminant ablation with minimal thermal diffusion

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning with adequate overlap

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning coverage

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm pulsed lasers in metal cleaning; starting at 100 W balances efficiency and safety for Mo's high reflectivity and thermal conductivity, avoiding melting (threshold ~300 W). Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and studies on refractory metals (e.g., J. Laser Appl. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contamination removal; 1500 mm/s optimal for light contaminants on Mo, slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy; ensures uniform cleaning without excessive heat buildup. Source: Laserax equipment manuals and industry standards for IR laser cleaning.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of reflective metals like Mo; 30 ns minimizes HAZ compared to longer pulses, while ps/fs (not typical at 1064 nm for industrial) reduce thermal effects further. Source: Laser physics reviews (Opt. Lasers Eng. 2020).

repetition_rate.description: Standard for pulsed cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for Mo to achieve fast processing without significant heat accumulation due to its conductivity; higher rates (>50 kHz) increase speed but risk thermal damage. Source: Academic literature on pulse train effects (Appl. Surf. Sci. 2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence for contaminant ablation without substrate damage; Mo's ablation threshold ~3-5 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to reflectivity; 1.5 J/cm² safe for cleaning. Conservative estimate from similar metals. Source: Ablation threshold studies (J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. 2017).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise industrial cleaning; 200 μm recommended for Mo surface prep, balancing resolution for fine details and coverage for larger areas. Source: Manufacturer specs (e.g., Coherent Armo series) and process optimization.

pass_count.description: Typically 1-3 passes for complete removal on metals; 2 passes recommended for Mo to ensure thorough cleaning of oxides/contaminants without overexposure. Source: Industry case studies on refractory metal processing (Laser Cleaning Handbook, 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 50% optimal for Mo to avoid gaps or uneven heating, adjustable higher for heavy contamination. Source: Process optimization studies (Opt. Laser Technol. 2022).
